tools.name: datasets.bigqueryDatasetsDelete
tools.description: Deletes the dataset specified by the datasetId value. Before you can delete a dataset, you must delete all its tables, either manually or by specifying deleteContents. Immediately after deletion, you can create another dataset with the same name.

tools.name: datasets.bigqueryDatasetsGet
tools.description: Returns the dataset specified by datasetID.

tools.name: datasets.bigqueryDatasetsInsert
tools.description: Creates a new empty dataset.

tools.name: datasets.bigqueryDatasetsList
tools.description: Lists all datasets in the specified project to which you have been granted the READER dataset role.

tools.name: datasets.bigqueryDatasetsPatch
tools.description: Updates information in an existing dataset. The update method replaces the entire dataset resource, whereas the patch method only replaces fields that are provided in the submitted dataset resource. This method supports patch semantics.

tools.name: datasets.bigqueryDatasetsUpdate
tools.description: Updates information in an existing dataset. The update method replaces the entire dataset resource, whereas the patch method only replaces fields that are provided in the submitted dataset resource.

tools.name: jobs.bigqueryJobsCancel
tools.description: Requests that a job be cancelled. This call will return immediately, and the client will need to poll for the job status to see if the cancel completed successfully. Cancelled jobs may still incur costs.

tools.name: jobs.bigqueryJobsDelete
tools.description: Requests the deletion of the metadata of a job. This call returns when the job's metadata is deleted.

tools.name: jobs.bigqueryJobsGet
tools.description: Returns information about a specific job. Job information is available for a six month period after creation. Requires that you're the person who ran the job, or have the Is Owner project role.

tools.name: jobs.bigqueryJobsGetQueryResults
tools.description: Retrieves the results of a query job.

tools.name: jobs.bigqueryJobsInsert
tools.description: Starts a new asynchronous job. Requires the Can View project role.

tools.name: jobs.bigqueryJobsList
tools.description: Lists all jobs that you started in the specified project. Job information is available for a six month period after creation. The job list is sorted in reverse chronological order, by job creation time. Requires the Can View project role, or the Is Owner project role if you set the allUsers property.

tools.name: jobs.bigqueryJobsQuery
tools.description: Runs a BigQuery SQL query synchronously and returns query results if the query completes within a specified timeout.

tools.name: models.bigqueryModelsDelete
tools.description: Deletes the model specified by modelId from the dataset.

tools.name: models.bigqueryModelsGet
tools.description: Gets the specified model resource by model ID.

tools.name: models.bigqueryModelsList
tools.description: Lists all models in the specified dataset. Requires the READER dataset role. After retrieving the list of models, you can get information about a particular model by calling the models.get method.

tools.name: models.bigqueryModelsPatch
tools.description: Patch specific fields in the specified model.

tools.name: projects.bigqueryProjectsGetServiceAccount
tools.description: Returns the email address of the service account for your project used for interactions with Google Cloud KMS.

tools.name: projects.bigqueryProjectsList
tools.description: Lists all projects to which you have been granted any project role.

tools.name: routines.bigqueryRoutinesDelete
tools.description: Deletes the routine specified by routineId from the dataset.

tools.name: routines.bigqueryRoutinesGet
tools.description: Gets the specified routine resource by routine ID.

tools.name: routines.bigqueryRoutinesInsert
tools.description: Creates a new routine in the dataset.

tools.name: routines.bigqueryRoutinesList
tools.description: Lists all routines in the specified dataset. Requires the READER dataset role.

tools.name: routines.bigqueryRoutinesUpdate
tools.description: Updates information in an existing routine. The update method replaces the entire Routine resource.

tools.name: rowAccessPolicies.bigqueryRowAccessPoliciesList
tools.description: Lists all row access policies on the specified table.

tools.name: tabledata.bigqueryTabledataInsertAll
tools.description: Streams data into BigQuery one record at a time without needing to run a load job. Requires the WRITER dataset role.

tools.name: tabledata.bigqueryTabledataList
tools.description: Retrieves table data from a specified set of rows. Requires the READER dataset role.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesDelete
tools.description: Deletes the table specified by tableId from the dataset. If the table contains data, all the data will be deleted.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesGet
tools.description: Gets the specified table resource by table ID. This method does not return the data in the table, it only returns the table resource, which describes the structure of this table.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesGetIamPolicy
tools.description: Gets the access control policy for a resource. Returns an empty policy if the resource exists and does not have a policy set.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesInsert
tools.description: Creates a new, empty table in the dataset.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesList
tools.description: Lists all tables in the specified dataset. Requires the READER dataset role.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesPatch
tools.description: Updates information in an existing table. The update method replaces the entire table resource, whereas the patch method only replaces fields that are provided in the submitted table resource. This method supports patch semantics.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesSetIamPolicy
tools.description: Sets the access control policy on the specified resource. Replaces any existing policy. Can return `NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_ARGUMENT`, and `PERMISSION_DENIED` errors.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesTestIamPermissions
tools.description: Returns permissions that a caller has on the specified resource. If the resource does not exist, this will return an empty set of permissions, not a `NOT_FOUND` error. Note: This operation is designed to be used for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization checking. This operation may "fail open" without warning.

tools.name: tables.bigqueryTablesUpdate
tools.description: Updates information in an existing table. The update method replaces the entire table resource, whereas the patch method only replaces fields that are provided in the submitted table resource.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
